metadata.title: SYNGO_MF_122
metadata.comment: Rationale: Three complementary methods were used to demonstrate that Bassoon regulates the number of calcium channels at the ribbon synapse. Immunofluorescence and STED microsopy demonstrated a reduced abundance of calcium channel signal at ribbons (Figure 2). electrophysiology and live imaging showed that this leads to a reduction of calcium influx (figure 3 and 4). Note that the mouse used in this study is only a partial knockout, and that a similar phenotype was observed in cultured neuron of a full knockout (Davydova et.al 2014).. Experimental description: Mice with deletion of exons 4 and 5 of the Bassoon gene (BsnΔEx4/5; Altrock et al., 2003) or carrying a gene-trapped allele (Bsngt, Lexicon Pharmaceuticals, Inc.), and wild-type littermates were used.
